author: rādhāvallabha dāsa
verses: āre mora ācārya ṭhākura dayāra sāgara⋅vara, jagabhari vithārala, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa līlā⋅rasa pūra gaurāṅga⋅cādera hena, nirupama⋅guṇa⋅gaṇa, dvijarāja gauḍa⋅bhuvane malla⋅bhūpati ādi, hari⋅rase unamādi, bhela jā’ra karuṇā⋅kiraṇe jatna kariyā ati, rasa⋅līlā⋅ngraha tathi, vṛndāvana bhūmi saṇe (sane) āni rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa rasa⋅līlā, deśe deśe pracārilā, āsvādana kariyā āpani emana dayāla pahu, cakṣu bhari nā dekhilu, hṛdaye rahala śela phuṭi e rādhāvallabha dāsa, kare mane abhilāṣa, kabe se dekhiba pada du’ṭī
en_translation:
Oh, my Advaita Ācārya! He is the best ocean of mercy, who has spread the nectar of the pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa throughout the world.

Like Lord Gaurāṅga, he has incomparable qualities and is the King of the twice-born in the land of Gauḍa. Even great kings became intoxicated with the nectar of devotion to Lord Hari by the rays of his mercy.

With great effort, he compiled the pastimes of divine love and brought the mood of Vṛndāvana to this world. He preached the pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in every land, tasting them himself.

Such a merciful Master I have not seen to my heart's content; a spear remains pierced in my heart. This Rādhāvallabha Dāsa cherishes a desire in his mind: "When will I see his two lotus feet?"
